assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response correctly states Regular channel clusters will automatically upgrade to the next available version (likely 1.31+) shortly after EoS date

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states Extended channel gets 'up to 12 additional months' instead of the correct 24 months total support period

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters face systematic node-level EoS enforcement — nodes on EoS versions are force-upgraded
assertions.reason: Response mentions No channel clusters will be upgraded but doesn't specifically explain the node-level EoS enforcement mechanism

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: No mention of maintenance exclusions or the ability to temporarily defer EoS force-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends proactive manual upgrades and testing before EoS hits, with specific strategy recommendations

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: No mention of Cloud Logging notifications or EoS notifications as a planning tool

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: No mention of deprecation insights dashboard or API compatibility checking tools

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: No mention of snowflaking concept or warnings about manually freezing versions and associated risks
